rationale_for_baseline_tier: Tinea pedis with fissuring but no secondary infection is benign and managed at home with a topical antifungal and moisture control, with routine review as fissures heal (T1). Secondary cellulitis / diabetic-foot infection routes to GP/ED (T4).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness spreading up the foot/leg as a hot painful patch, a red streak (lymphangitis), thick yellow pus from the fissures, or a fever — especially in a diabetic foot
escalation_edges.rationale: Secondary cellulitis / diabetic-foot infection needing antibiotics ± urgent care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads up your foot or leg as a hot painful patch, you get a red streak, the cracks leak thick yellow pus, or you get a fever — see a doctor / go to ED that day.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Diabetes/immunosuppression (not present) would lower the threshold
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: If diabetic, treat foot infections urgently and inspect the feet daily.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Use the antifungal cream and antiseptic soaks, dry carefully between your toes, wear cotton socks and rotate your shoes. The itch settles in a few days and the cracks heal over a week or so.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care + GP review as fissures heal; GP/ED for spreading cellulitis
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: GP/ED for spreading redness up the foot/leg, streaking, pus or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Tinea pedis (interdigital/moccasin) with fissures; secondary cellulitis/diabetes screened negative; topical antifungal + antiseptic soaks + moisture/footwear control; cellulitis safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without a secondary-cellulitis safety-net for fissured feet.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Bacteria enter the fissures and cause an ascending cellulitis (or diabetic-foot infection) that is missed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No moisture/footwear advice, so it persists/recurs.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/oral treatment for uncomplicated athlete's foot — unnecessary.
